Title: Biosynthesis, elicitation and roles of monocot terpenoid phytoalexins

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-elicited with momilactone cluster genes after chitin elicitation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsMAS was biochemically characterized as momilactone A synthase. 2. It converts 3β-hydroxy-9βH-pimaradiene-19,6β-olide into momilactone A in vitro. 3. Its transcript is co-elicited with clustered momilactone biosynthetic genes, supporting a dedicated role in momilactone biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering enhanced momilactone production, especially in combination with OsCPS4/OsKSL4/OsCYP99A3; caution that defense and allelopathic effects may alter rhizosphere interactions.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro enzyme assay; co-expression/co-elicitation analysis
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although enzyme function is catalytic, this paper explicitly names it as a synthase completing a late pathway step; classified here as Common_Gene per template allowance for non-core enzyme-class exceptions if needed.

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCEBiP binds chitin oligosaccharides and is required for full chitin-triggered defense signaling. 2. RNAi lines lacking OsCEBiP show strongly reduced ROS and increased fungal invasion. 3. It acts upstream of diterpenoid phytoalexin accumulation via chitin perception together with OsCERK1.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to strengthen elicitor-triggered phytoalexin induction and disease resistance.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Biochemical ligand binding; RNAi; pathogen infection assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes elicitor-induced momilactone and phytocassane accumulation indirectly through perception signaling
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Receptor lacks intracellular signaling domain and functions in receptor complex.

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCERK1 forms elicitor-induced hetero-oligomeric complexes with OsCEBiP. 2. RNAi suppression of OsCERK1 severely suppresses (GlcNAc)8-induced momilactone and phytocassane accumulation. 3. It acts as a central receptor kinase linking chitin perception to downstream phytoalexin signaling.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for improving inducible defense metabolism through enhanced receptor signaling.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi; elicitor treatment; phytoalexin quantification; complex formation assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates elicitor-induced diterpenoid phytoalexin accumulation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of plasma membrane defensome complex.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Activated within minutes of chitin perception
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsRac1 is a defensome-associated small GTPase activated after chitin perception. 2. Constitutive expression causes increased H2O2, cell death, OsCPS2 transcript accumulation and higher momilactone A levels. 3. OsRac1 therefore positively links early immune signaling to phytoalexin biosynthetic gene induction.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Cell death-associated lesions may impose fitness costs
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful signaling target to potentiate inducible phytoalexin defenses, but constitutive activation may incur lesion or growth penalties.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Constitutive overexpression; ROS assays; transcript analysis; metabolite analysis
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts downstream of OsCERK1/OsCEBiP and upstream of ROS-related signaling.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Required for TvX-induced Ca2+ response and downstream gene induction
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsTPC1 partly mediates TvX-induced cytosolic Ca2+ elevation. 2. ostpc1 mutants show reduced induction of OsCPS2, OsCPS4, OsKSL4 and OsKSL7 and reduced diterpenoid phytoalexin accumulation. 3. Transgenic complementation with wild-type OsTPC1 restores these responses.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to strengthen elicitor-responsive phytoalexin accumulation without directly altering pathway enzymes.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; transgenic complementation; Ca2+ assays; transcript and metabolite measurements
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates TvX-induced diterpenoid phytoalexin production
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts in early Ca2+-dependent signaling rather than directly in metabolism.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcripts accumulate after TvX and (GlcNAc)7 elicitation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCIPK14 and OsCIPK15 are induced by TvX and chitin elicitation. 2. RNAi silencing reduces TvX-induced cell death, phytoalexin gene expression and accumulation of momilactones/phytocassanes. 3. Overexpression of OsCIPK15 enhances TvX-elicited phytoalexin accumulation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for increasing elicitor-responsive phytoalexin production; preferable in inducible contexts.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi; overexpression; interaction assays with OsCBL4; elicitor treatments; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The two kinases are highly similar and were largely studied together.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Constitutive and wound-inducible JA/JA-Ile production depends on OsAOC
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsAOC is required for jasmonate biosynthesis in rice. 2. Osaoc mutants are deficient in JA/JA-Ile and accumulate less momilactone after Magnaporthe infection. 3. Phytocassane accumulation is unaffected, showing branch-specific jasmonate dependence.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be leveraged to modulate jasmonate-dependent momilactone production, but perturbation may broadly alter defense and development.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutants; hormone measurements; pathogen infection; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for full fungal-induced momilactone accumulation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts indirectly through JA biosynthesis, not as a phytoalexin pathway enzyme.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Mutant Osjar1-2 abolishes exogenous JA-induced but not all pathogen-induced diterpenoid phytoalexin accumulation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsJAR1 is required to convert JA into JA-Ile for canonical jasmonate signaling. 2. Osjar1-2 suppresses exogenous JA-induced accumulation of momilactones and phytocassanes, but JA-Ile restores the response. 3. Pathogen- or stress-induced diterpenoid phytoalexin accumulation can still occur, demonstrating jasmonate-dependent and -independent pathways.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altering OsJAR1 may broadly affect jasmonate-regulated traits
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Highlights the need to consider both JA-Ile-dependent and independent branches when engineering defense metabolism.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; exogenous JA and JA-Ile treatments; metabolite analysis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for JA-induced, but not all endogenous stress-induced, diterpenoid phytoalexin accumulation
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Heavy metal stress; Magnaporthe infection; exogenous JA or JA-Ile
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Demonstrates existence of parallel signaling routes controlling rice diterpenoid phytoalexins.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly inducible in silk after Fusarium graminearum infection; induced in stems by Ostrinia nubilalis and Rhizopus microsporus
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly inducible in challenged maize tissues
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Differential display transcript profiling; accession NA
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmAn2 encodes the maize ent-CPS and is highly inducible by fungal infection and herbivory. 2. Kauralexins are hypothesized to be predominant downstream products of ZmAn2. 3. The gene is considered a major entry point to maize diterpenoid phytoalexin biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for boosting inducible kauralexin production and stem defense in maize.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; enzyme function confirmation; inducible expression analysis; metabolite correlation
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fusarium graminearum infection; Ostrinia nubilalis herbivory; Rhizopus microsporus inoculation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although enzymatic, the review highlights it as a key focal maize phytoalexin gene; retained here due to centrality and as bridge to kauralexin pathway.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: MEP pathway supplying diterpenoid phytoalexin precursors
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses pyruvate and glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate to form 1-deoxy-D-xylulose 5-phosphate
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step supported by inhibitor sensitivity and coordinated induction
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Momilactones; phytocassanes; other rice diterpenoid phytoalexins
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript accumulates synchronously in elicitor-induced rice cells
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsDXS3 is among seven MEP-pathway genes co-induced during elicitor-triggered phytoalexin production. 2. Chemical inhibition of DXS suppresses rice diterpenoid phytoalexin accumulation. 3. OsTGAP1 overexpression correlates with increased OsDXS3 expression.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Perturbation may affect broader plastidial isoprenoid metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful to enhance precursor supply for phytoalexin engineering, but should be balanced to avoid broad metabolic disruption.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcript co-induction; inhibitor treatment; transcription factor overexpression correlation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases precursor supply for diterpenoid phytoalexin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review treats the MEP pathway as source of GGPP for diterpenoid phytoalexins.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: MEP pathway supplying diterpenoid phytoalexin precursors
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step supported by inhibitor sensitivity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsDXR is co-induced with other MEP genes in elicited rice cells. 2. Inhibition of DXR suppresses diterpenoid phytoalexin accumulation. 3. This supports MEP-pathway dependence of rice phytoalexin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broad effects on plastidial isoprenoids expected
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential precursor-supply engineering node with caution due to pleiotropy.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes precursor supply for diterpenoid phytoalexins

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: ent-CDP branch leading to phytocassanes and oryzalexins A-F
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-entry enzyme for phytoalexin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Inducible by UV and chitin elicitation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Elicitor-inducible; increased with OsRac1 activation and reduced in ostpc1 signaling mutants
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCPS2 produces ent-CDP dedicated to diterpenoid phytoalexin rather than GA biosynthesis. 2. It forms a chromosome 2 biosynthetic cluster with OsKSL7 and CYP genes. 3. Its transcript is induced by elicitation and linked to phytocassane production.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong engineering target for enhancing ent-CDP-derived rice phytoalexins, ideally stacked with downstream KSL/CYP genes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cDNA cloning; biochemical enzyme assay; expression induction; genomic clustering
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: UV irradiation; chitin elicitation; TvX signaling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Distinct from OsCPS1, which is required for GA metabolism.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: syn-CDP branch leading to momilactones and oryzalexin S
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-entry enzyme for momilactone pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by UV and elicitation; basal expression in roots/shoots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reduced in ostpc1 mutants; induced during defense
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCPS4 produces syn-CDP and is closely linked genomically and functionally with OsKSL4 in the momilactone cluster. 2. Mutants lacking OsCPS4 lose momilactone production and show reduced allelopathy. 3. Some studies report increased pathogen susceptibility in Oscps4 under low inoculum, while others found little change, indicating subtle defense phenotypes.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Oscps4 mutants have reduced allelopathic capacity; altered seedling ecology
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Prime target for engineering momilactone production and allelopathic traits; deployment should consider ecological effects on neighboring plants.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Biochemical characterization; mutant analysis; metabolite profiling; allelopathy assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for momilactone biosynthesis and contributes to allelopathy
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: UV irradiation; chitin elicitation; Magnaporthe infection
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Part of chromosome 4 momilactone biosynthetic cluster.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: syn-CDP to momilactone olefin precursor branch
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate hydrocarbon precursor of momilactones
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key pathway enzyme for momilactone branch
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Elicitor inducible; high promoter responsiveness to chitin-related signals
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by elicitation; positively regulated by OsTGAP1; reduced in ostpc1 mutants
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray/transcript profiling; promoter-luciferase analysis; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsKSL4 converts syn-CDP to the momilactone precursor syn-pimara-7,15-diene. 2. Its promoter contains an elicitor-responsive TGACG motif targeted by OsTGAP1-related regulation. 3. Knockout mutants lacking OsKSL4 lose momilactone production and show reduced allelopathic effects.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Oskls4 mutants show ~50% reduction in seed germination in one study; altered physiology may arise from precursor accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for breeding or engineering stronger momilactone-based defense/allelopathy, but precursor build-up and germination effects should be monitored.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Biochemical enzyme assay; promoter deletion/mutation assay; knockout mutant analysis; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native OsKSL4 promoter with elicitor-responsive TGACG motif
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for momilactone biosynthesis and contributes to allelopathic activity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Chromosome 4 cluster member adjacent to OsCPS4.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key pathway enzyme for phytocassane branch
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Co-regulated with clustered CYPs and positively influenced by OsTGAP1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsKSL7 forms ent-cassa-12,15-diene from ent-CDP. 2. It is genomically clustered with OsCPS2 and multiple CYPs involved in phytocassane biosynthesis. 3. Its transcript is elicitor-inducible and correlates with phytocassane production.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Good target for increasing phytocassane-based disease resistance, especially with clustered CYPs.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cDNA cloning; biochemical enzyme assay; expression co-regulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Located in chromosome 2 diterpenoid biosynthetic cluster.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: syn-CDP to oryzalexin S precursor branch
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in elicitor-treated cells; transcript induced by lipopolysaccharide
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsKSL8 acts on syn-CDP to yield syn-stemar-13-ene, the precursor of oryzalexin S. 2. Its transcript is induced by bacterial lipopolysaccharide treatment. 3. The connection to actual oryzalexin S accumulation after LPS remains unresolved.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential route to expand phytoalexin diversity, though downstream steps remain less resolved.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Biochemical enzyme assay; transcript induction analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports biosynthesis of the oryzalexin S precursor

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: ent-CDP to oryzalexin A-F precursor branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts ent-copalyl diphosphate to ent-sandaracopimaradiene; can also react with syn-CDP to produce syn-labda-8(14),15-diene
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms precursor of oryzalexins D/E and related compounds
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsKSL10 produces ent-sandaracopimaradiene, precursor to oryzalexins. 2. It also shows additional activity with syn-CDP, indicating catalytic promiscuity. 3. Its product is further oxidized by OsCYP701A8 and OsCYP76M6/8 to complete oryzalexin D/E formation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering the oryzalexin branch; promiscuity may create additional diterpene products.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Displays substrate promiscuity with both ent-CDP and syn-CDP.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: C19 oxidation module of momilactone pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes three-step oxidation of the C19 methyl group of syn-pimara-7,15-diene to the corresponding C19 carboxylic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms advanced intermediate in target-product pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCYP99A3 specifically oxidizes the C19 methyl of syn-pimara-7,15-diene through three steps to syn-pimara-7,15-dien-19-oic acid. 2. RNAi co-suppression of OsCYP99A2/A3 suppresses momilactone accumulation but not phytocassanes. 3. This establishes a dedicated role in momilactone biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important enzyme for engineering the momilactone branch; should be stacked with upstream synthases and OsMAS.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi knockdown; heterologous biochemical assay; metabolite analysis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: OsCYP99A2/A3 co-suppression phenotype supports shared function, but OsCYP99A3 enzymology is better defined.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates ent-cassa-12,15-diene at the C11α position
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms direct oxidized intermediate for phytocassane biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-regulated with OsCPS2 and OsKSL7 by chitin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCYP76M7 catalyzes C11α hydroxylation of ent-cassa-12,15-diene. 2. This reaction matches structural features of phytocassanes and corresponding intermediates found in planta. 3. Together with OsCYP76M8 it is implicated in phytocassane biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Suitable downstream engineering target for phytocassane production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Biochemical enzyme assay; transcript co-regulation; metabolite structural correspondence
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functionally overlaps with OsCYP76M8 for C11α hydroxylation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes C11α hydroxylation of ent-cassa-12,15-diene, C6β hydroxylation of syn-pimara-7,15-diene, and C7β hydroxylation of ent-sandaracopimaradiene
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms oxidized intermediates in multiple phytoalexin branches
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-regulated with phytocassane cluster genes after chitin elicitation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCYP76M8 is catalytically promiscuous and hydroxylates intermediates from several diterpenoid branches. 2. RNAi silencing reduced phytocassane production, although co-suppression of OsCYP76M7 complicates assignment. 3. Its biochemical promiscuity suggests possible roles in momilactone and oryzalexin biosynthesis, but direct in planta evidence is strongest for phytocassanes.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Promiscuity may redirect multiple diterpenoid branches
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential multiplex engineering node for broadening phytoalexin profiles, but promiscuity could create unintended products.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi; biochemical enzyme assay; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to phytocassane accumulation and may affect other phytoalexin branches
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence for momilactone/oryzalexin roles remains indirect.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates ent-cassa-12,15-diene at the C2 position
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms later intermediate toward C2-oxygenated phytocassanes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-regulated with OsCPS2 and OsKSL7 by chitin elicitation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCYP71Z7 catalyzes C2 hydroxylation of ent-cassa-12,15-diene. 2. Knockdown specifically suppresses accumulation of C2-oxygenated phytocassanes. 3. The relatively high Km suggests it may act in later pathway context.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for tuning specific phytocassane structural variants rather than total phytoalexin flux.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Modest substrate affinity suggests contextual pathway/channeling effects.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates ent-sandaracopimaradiene and ent-cassa-12,15-diene at C3
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms common oxidized intermediate for oryzalexin and phytocassane branches
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCYP701A8 efficiently C3-hydroxylates both ent-sandaracopimaradiene and ent-cassa-12,15-diene. 2. This reaction is consistent with biosynthesis of oryzalexins and phytocassanes. 3. It helps define the first complete rice phytoalexin pathways when combined with downstream CYP76M enzymes.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising shared-node enzyme for engineering multiple phytoalexin classes simultaneously.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Biochemical enzyme assay; elicitor-induced transcript analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes biosynthesis of both oryzalexins and phytocassanes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Formerly discussed as KOL due to similarity with GA ent-kaurene oxidase.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes downstream hydroxylation in the ent-sandaracopimaradiene branch, including C9β hydroxylation leading to oryzalexin E; also catalyzes C7β hydroxylation of ent-sandaracopimaradiene
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms near-terminal products of oryzalexin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsCYP76M6 catalyzes final hydroxylation steps in oryzalexin biosynthesis. 2. Together with OsCYP701A8, it completes the pathway to oryzalexin E. 3. The work defined one of the first complete rice diterpenoid phytoalexin pathways.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for generating specific bioactive oryzalexin end products.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also reported to catalyze C7β hydroxylation of ent-sandaracopimaradiene.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts farnesyl diphosphate to (S)-β-bisabolene and cyclized (S)-β-macrocarpene
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-entry enzyme inferred from transcript-metabolite correlation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced by Ustilago maydis, Spodoptera littoralis herbivory, fungi, and Ostrinia nubilalis attack
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Inducible in leaves, stems, roots and shoots depending on challenge
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmTPS6 encodes a sesquiterpene synthase producing β-macrocarpene and β-bisabolene from FPP. 2. Suppression of ZmTps6/11 increases fungal growth, supporting a role in zealexin-mediated defense. 3. Transcript abundance correlates very strongly with zealexin accumulation across fungal elicitors.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for enhancing maize zealexin-based disease resistance; likely best used with ZmTPS11 and downstream oxidation capacity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; heterologous enzyme assay; RNAi/VIGS; transcript-metabolite correlation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes zealexin precursor formation and disease resistance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Frequently considered jointly with the nearly identical ZmTPS11.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts farnesyl diphosphate to β-macrocarpene and β-bisabolene
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Elicited by herbivory and fungal attack
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmTPS11 is nearly identical to ZmTPS6 and produces the same sesquiterpene precursors. 2. Co-suppression of ZmTps6/11 enhances U. maydis growth. 3. Expression correlates with zealexin accumulation across eliciting fungi.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be combined with ZmTPS6 to increase zealexin capacity and disease defense.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene cloning; heterologous enzyme assay; RNAi/VIGS; transcript-metabolite correlation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes zealexin precursor formation and pathogen resistance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The two genes were mostly functionally analyzed together.

Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray/transcript profiling; promoter-luciferase analysis; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Targets OsKSL4 promoter TGACG motif; influences OsKSL4; OsKSL7; OsDXS3
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native OsKSL4 promoter with elicitor-responsive TGACG motif
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator of multiple diterpenoid phytoalexin classes, particularly momilactones and phytocassanes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances elicitor-induced accumulation of momilactones and phytocassanes
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: OsKSL4; OsKSL7; OsDXS3; five clustered momilactone biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsTGAP1 was identified as a TGA bZIP transcription factor required for full elicitor-induced momilactone production. 2. Tos17 insertion mutants show reduced momilactones, while overexpression enhances both momilactones and phytocassanes. 3. OsTGAP1 upregulates MEP-pathway and diterpenoid biosynthetic genes, and is constitutively high in roots that secrete momilactones.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter deletion/mutation assay; Tos17 mutant analysis; overexpression; transcript profiling; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as a positive regulator opposite to OsWRKY76.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Constitutively elevated in roots; inducible by elicitation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates/positively influences OsKSL4, OsKSL7, OsDXS3 and clustered momilactone genes
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent regulatory target to coordinately increase multiple phytoalexin branches, especially for inducible defense or root exudate traits.

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Negative regulator that suppresses phytoalexin accumulation and increases pathogen susceptibility
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression increases susceptibility to Magnaporthe oryzae
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppresses genes involved in phytoalexin biosynthesis and lowers diterpenoid accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: M. oryzae-elicited phytoalexin biosynthetic genes (individual direct targets not specified)
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsWRKY76 transcripts accumulate after wounding and benzothiadiazole treatment. 2. Overexpressing lines suppress M. oryzae-induced phytoalexin biosynthetic genes. 3. Reduced diterpenoid accumulation in these lines is associated with increased pathogen susceptibility.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; transcript profiling; metabolite analysis; pathogen susceptibility assays
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts opposite to OsTGAP1 in transcriptional control of rice phytoalexins.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Genome editing or downregulation may relieve repression and enhance defense metabolite accumulation, but broader WRKY effects should be considered.
